name: cc-skill-strategic-compact
capabilities: Track cumulative tool call counts across Claude Code session invocations
Output strategic compaction suggestions to stderr at configurable thresholds
Integrate with Claude hooks for automatic PreToolUse triggering
Identify workflow transition points for optimal context preservation
Support customizable threshold via environment variable
Provide non-intrusive suggestions without interrupting active tasks
author: affaan-m
securitySummary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a benign shell script that tracks tool call counts in a temp file and outputs suggestions to stderr. No network access, no credential handling, no command injection vectors. The temp file usage is for local state tracking only.
valueStatement: Long Claude Code sessions accumulate context that degrades performance over time. This skill identifies optimal moments to compact context without disrupting workflow momentum.
limitations: Requires bash shell environment to execute
Counter state stored in temp file may not persist across system reboots
Does not automatically compact context - only provides suggestions
Hook configuration must be manually added to Claude settings

useCases.targetUser: Software engineers using Claude Code for extended coding sessions
useCases.title: Extended Development Sessions
useCases.description: Developers working on complex multi-file refactoring tasks receive timely reminders to compact context after completing exploration phases before moving to implementation

useCases.targetUser: Data professionals leveraging AI for analytical workflows
useCases.title: Research to Implementation Transitions
useCases.description: Data scientists and analysts get checkpoint notifications when transitioning from data exploration to code generation, ensuring relevant context is preserved

useCases.targetUser: Technical project managers coordinating AI-assisted development
useCases.title: Multi-Task Workflow Management
useCases.description: Project managers juggling multiple concurrent tasks receive periodic suggestions to compact completed work before starting new milestones

promptTemplates.title: Basic Compaction Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Review our conversation and create a concise summary of key decisions, code changes, and open questions. Preserve only what's needed to continue the current task.
promptTemplates.scenario: After receiving a compaction suggestion, use this template to manually compact context while preserving essential information

promptTemplates.title: Phase Transition Summary
promptTemplates.prompt: We are transitioning from exploration to implementation. Summarize the requirements and constraints discovered, then reset context for the implementation phase.
promptTemplates.scenario: When the skill signals a workflow phase change, use this to preserve discoveries while clearing exploration context

promptTemplates.title: Milestone Completion Archive
promptTemplates.prompt: Complete the current milestone and create an archival summary. Document what was accomplished, files modified, and any follow-up actions. Then compact to prepare for the next milestone.
promptTemplates.scenario: At major checkpoint intervals (every 25 tool calls after threshold), archive completed work before moving forward

promptTemplates.title: Context Hygiene Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze our current conversation context. Identify redundant exchanges, resolved questions, and outdated information that can be safely removed while maintaining task continuity.
promptTemplates.scenario: Proactive context audit when session feels sluggish or after receiving multiple compaction suggestions

bestPractices: Compact context after completing logical workflow phases, not during active problem-solving
Review compaction suggestions and manually verify important context is preserved before compacting
Set COMPACT_THRESHOLD environment variable to match your typical session complexity and attention span
antiPatterns: Do not compact context in the middle of debugging sessions where full history is essential
Avoid setting thresholds too low (under 25) which causes premature compaction suggestions
Do not rely solely on automated suggestions - use judgment about task criticality before compacting

faq.question: How does this skill track tool call counts?
faq.answer: It maintains a simple counter file in the /tmp directory. Each hook invocation increments the counter, allowing the script to track cumulative tool usage across the session.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically compact my context?
faq.answer: No, it only provides suggestions by writing to stderr. You must manually trigger compaction using the /compact command or by summarizing context yourself when you receive a suggestion.

faq.question: Can I customize when suggestions appear?
faq.answer: Yes, set the COMPACT_THRESHOLD environment variable to your preferred tool call count (default is 50). After the threshold, suggestions appear every 25 tool calls.

faq.question: What happens if I delete the temp counter file?
faq.answer: The script will create a new file and start counting from 1. You will receive suggestions based on the new count, effectively resetting the tracking for your current session.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The script has no network access, does not read project files, and only tracks an integer count. The temp file contains only a number with no project-specific data.

faq.question: Do I need to configure hooks manually?
faq.answer: Yes. Add the hook configuration shown in the script comments to your ~/.claude/settings.json file to enable automatic suggestions before Edit or Write tool invocations.
